steps.title: Calculate vitamin B12 per ounce for each food
steps.content: Cheese provides 1 microgram per 3.0 ounces, so the rate is $\frac{1}{3.0}$ micrograms per ounce. Tuna provides 1 microgram per 1.2 ounces, so the rate is $\frac{1}{1.2}$ micrograms per ounce.

steps.title: Set up the equation based on total B12 consumed
steps.content: If x ounces of cheese are consumed and y ounces of tuna are consumed, the total B12 is: $$\frac{1}{3.0}x + \frac{1}{1.2}y = 1.7$$

steps.title: Convert to decimal form to match the choices
steps.content: $\frac{1}{3.0} \approx 0.33$ and $\frac{1}{1.2} \approx 0.83$, so the equation becomes $$0.33x + 0.83y = 1.7$$ That matches choice B — B is correct.
